A.label: Variant A

profiles.goal: Find an affordable two-night Lake Tahoe stay for a couple and decide which listing is worth investigating further.
profiles.task: On the Homes tab, search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests, compare the visible two-night prices and ratings, identify the best low-price option, and stop after recording whether the couple would continue to listing details.

constraints.budget_preference: lowest total visible price with acceptable rating

success_criteria: Search is submitted with Lake Tahoe and 2 guests.
Lowest visible price option is identified.
Rating and guest-favorite signals are considered.
No booking, account action, host message, or payment step is attempted.
frustration_triggers: Fees are not visible in the comparison view.
Guest count controls are unclear.
Low-price listings lack enough detail to judge value.

profiles.goal: Compare budget-friendly cabin options for a short weekend stay.
profiles.task: Search for Lake Tahoe with 2 guests, focus on cabin listings, compare Cabin in Kings Beach and Cabin in South Lake Tahoe by visible price, rating, and Guest favorite signal, then stop with a preferred option.

constraints.budget_preference: prefer cabin under the higher visible cabin price

success_criteria: Relevant cabin listings are found or noted from visible cards.
Price difference is considered before rating.
A single preferred cabin is selected for further review.
Task stops at comparison or intent assessment.
frustration_triggers: Cabin filters are unavailable or hard to find.
Total cost is ambiguous.
Guest favorite badge does not explain why it matters.

profiles.goal: Decide whether a higher-rated listing justifies paying more for a weekend trip.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests, compare the $498 Cabin in Kings Beach against the $588 Condo in South Lake Tahoe, inspect any available listing details, and stop after deciding whether the cheaper cabin is sufficient.

constraints.budget_preference: balance visible price against rating

success_criteria: Both target listings are compared.
Visible rating and price are captured.
Any missing fee or detail information is noted.
No checkout submission or personal data entry occurs.
frustration_triggers: Listing detail navigation is unclear.
Taxes or cleaning fees are hidden.
Price comparison requires too many clicks.

profiles.goal: Quickly assess if Staybnb can support a spontaneous low-cost weekend plan.
profiles.task: Use the search controls to enter Lake Tahoe, keep the trip to 2 guests, scan the first carousel of popular homes, and stop after rating confidence in finding a stay under a budget-conscious threshold.

constraints.budget_preference: avoid listings above midrange visible prices

success_criteria: Search path is completed without account creation.
At least three visible listings are price-compared.
Confidence to continue is assessed.
No wishlist action is used if it requires login.
frustration_triggers: Search results do not clearly update.
Budget options are mixed with expensive listings.
Checkout costs are not previewed early.

profiles.goal: Find a Lake Tahoe stay that looks suitable for a small family and worth deeper comparison.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 4 guests, compare visible listings for rating, price, and trust signals, then stop after choosing up to two listings for a family shortlist.

constraints.budget_preference: moderate price with strong trust signals

success_criteria: Guest count is adjusted to 4 if controls allow.
At least two listings are compared.
Guest favorite and rating signals influence the shortlist.
Task ends before booking or account creation.
frustration_triggers: Guest capacity is not visible on cards.
Family-relevant amenities are not obvious.
Fees and cancellation details are missing.

profiles.goal: Assess whether a higher-priced home is justified for a family stay.
profiles.task: Search for Lake Tahoe with 4 guests, compare Home in Carnelian Bay against lower-priced options, look for visible reasons to justify the price, and stop with a continue-or-abandon decision.

constraints.budget_preference: willing to pay more only for clear family value

success_criteria: Higher-priced home is compared against at least two cheaper options.
Rating and Guest favorite badge are recorded.
Missing family suitability information is noted.
No checkout submission is attempted.
frustration_triggers: Beds, rooms, or capacity are not visible early.
High price lacks explanation.
Trust signals are too vague.

profiles.goal: Create a cautious family shortlist without committing to a reservation.
profiles.task: Use Lake Tahoe and 3 guests in the search, inspect the visible listing cards, avoid login-only actions, and stop after selecting the safest-looking option for later review.

constraints.budget_preference: avoid extreme high prices unless trust is exceptional

success_criteria: Search uses 3 guests.
Listings are compared by price and rating.
A single safest-looking option is chosen.
No real personal data or payment details are entered.
frustration_triggers: Wishlist requires account access.
Safety or accessibility details are buried.
Checkout path appears before enough comparison detail.

profiles.goal: Determine whether the booking flow gives enough information for a family decision.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 5 guests, attempt to compare details for the visible homes, and stop after documenting whether the page provides enough information to continue toward mock checkout intent.

constraints.budget_preference: value-oriented but flexible for the right fit

success_criteria: Guest count is set or limitation is noted.
Comparison includes visible price, rating, and badge signals.
Readiness to continue is assessed without booking.
Any missing detail needed for a family is captured.
frustration_triggers: Guest controls do not support desired party size clearly.
Listing cards omit family capacity.
Mock checkout appears before full price clarity.

profiles.goal: Find a quiet, reliable Lake Tahoe stay for a short work trip.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 1 guest, compare listings by rating and visible professionalism signals, and stop after selecting the listing that seems most reliable for a solo work stay.

constraints.budget_preference: midrange acceptable if rating is strong

success_criteria: Search is completed for 1 guest.
Highest-confidence listing is chosen from visible cards.
Rating is prioritized over lowest price when justified.
No account, message, or payment action is attempted.
frustration_triggers: Workspace or Wi-Fi details are not visible.
Listing details require too many clicks.
Total price is unclear for expense planning.

profiles.goal: Quickly assess whether the site can support a reimbursable two-night business stay.
profiles.task: Use the search controls for Lake Tahoe and 1 guest, compare visible two-night prices, identify an option that would be easy to justify on expenses, and stop at intent assessment.

constraints.budget_preference: clear and defensible total price

success_criteria: Visible two-night prices are compared.
A reimbursable-looking option is identified.
Unclear fees are noted as expense risk.
No checkout submission or payment entry occurs.
frustration_triggers: Fees are hidden until late.
Search flow feels slower than expected.
Receipts or business details are not previewed.

profiles.goal: Choose between convenience and trust signals for a solo work stay.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 1 guest, compare the condo and cabin listings, inspect available details if reachable, and stop after deciding which one is more suitable for work travel.

constraints.budget_preference: reasonable price with high confidence

success_criteria: Condo and cabin options are compared.
Rating and Guest favorite status are used in the decision.
A work-travel suitability judgment is recorded.
No real personal information is submitted.
frustration_triggers: No business-friendly amenity information appears.
Guest favorite signal lacks context.
Mock checkout does not clarify final cost.

profiles.goal: Evaluate whether a quick booking flow feels trustworthy enough for a business traveler.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests to simulate a colleague trip, compare visible listings, proceed only as far as a non-submitted mock checkout intent screen if available, and stop before entering personal or payment data.

constraints.budget_preference: company-friendly price with strong rating

success_criteria: Search is completed for 2 guests.
A preferred listing is selected based on visible evidence.
Mock checkout intent is assessed without submission.
No account, host message, or payment detail is used.
frustration_triggers: Checkout path feels too committal.
Final price is not visible before intent assessment.
Trust and cancellation information are sparse.

profiles.goal: Explore whether Staybnb supports a memorable Lake Tahoe getaway beyond basic lodging.
profiles.task: Start on the Experiences tab if available, note what experience options or limitations appear, return to Homes if needed, compare distinctive Lake Tahoe stays, and stop after choosing the most interesting option to explore later.

constraints.budget_preference: flexible for distinctive stays

success_criteria: Experiences entry point is tried or its absence is noted.
At least two homes are compared for uniqueness and rating.
A most-interesting option is selected.
No booking or account action is performed.
frustration_triggers: Experiences tab does not expose useful content.
Listings feel generic.
Photos or detailed descriptions are unavailable.

profiles.goal: Find a high-rated cabin that feels special enough for a short trip.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests, compare cabin listings first, use ratings and Guest favorite badges to decide which cabin feels most appealing, and stop at comparison.

constraints.budget_preference: secondary to experience quality

success_criteria: Cabin listings are identified from visible cards.
Experience appeal is weighed with rating.
A preferred cabin is selected without checkout.
Missing experiential details are recorded.
frustration_triggers: No photos or atmosphere details are visible.
Only price and rating are available.
Comparison controls are too shallow.

profiles.goal: Assess whether a premium Lake Tahoe home looks worth the splurge for a memorable stay.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests, compare Home in Carnelian Bay with cheaper alternatives, look for visible trust or uniqueness signals, and stop after deciding whether to continue exploring it.

constraints.budget_preference: splurge acceptable if clearly justified

success_criteria: Premium listing is compared against at least two cheaper listings.
Rating and Guest favorite signals are considered.
Continue intent is assessed without purchase action.
No personal data or payment step is entered.
frustration_triggers: Premium price lacks visible justification.
Listing detail is too limited.
Fees are not transparent before intent assessment.

profiles.goal: Compare lodging versus new service or experience entry points without committing.
profiles.task: Click through Homes, Experiences, and Services tabs if present, search Lake Tahoe where applicable, then stop after noting which path feels most relevant for planning a getaway.

constraints.budget_preference: open-ended, experience-driven

success_criteria: Primary tabs are explored safely.
No login or sign-up flow is started.
A preferred planning path is identified.
Task remains at browsing and intent assessment only.
frustration_triggers: New tabs are labeled but not functional.
Navigation state is unclear.
Search context is lost between tabs.

profiles.goal: Understand whether the Staybnb search flow feels safe and clear before considering a stay.
profiles.task: Use the homepage controls to search Lake Tahoe for 1 guest, carefully inspect labels, visible prices, ratings, and footer trust links, then stop after deciding whether the site feels trustworthy enough to continue.

constraints.budget_preference: low risk over lowest price

success_criteria: Search controls are used without account creation.
Trust and safety or research standards links are noticed if visible.
At least one listing is evaluated for confidence.
No booking, host message, or payment step is attempted.
frustration_triggers: The flow asks for commitment too early.
Trust information is hard to find.
Total price is incomplete.

profiles.goal: Learn how guest count and search results work without making a reservation.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe, adjust Who to 2 guests if possible, observe how the results or listing cards respond, and stop after documenting whether the controls were understandable.

constraints.budget_preference: clear cost before preference

success_criteria: Guest control is attempted and result is observed.
Search is submitted or limitation is noted.
User confidence in the control flow is assessed.
No account, payment, or checkout submission occurs.
frustration_triggers: Guest selector labels are ambiguous.
Results do not visibly change.
Buttons look like they may trigger booking.

profiles.goal: Compare listings cautiously and avoid accidental booking actions.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 1 guest, open or inspect listing details only if clearly safe, avoid wishlist and account links, and stop after identifying the safest-looking listing to revisit.

constraints.budget_preference: prefer transparent, lower-risk options

success_criteria: At least two listings are compared cautiously.
Potentially account-gated actions are avoided.
A safest-looking listing is selected.
The task stops before mock checkout submission.
frustration_triggers: Wishlist icons are unclear.
Listing clicks appear to move toward checkout.
Safety and cancellation details are not obvious.

profiles.goal: Assess checkout intent without entering real information or making a booking.
profiles.task: Search Lake Tahoe for 2 guests, select the listing that looks most trustworthy from visible ratings and badges, proceed only if the mock checkout path is clearly non-final, and stop immediately before any submit, personal data, or payment step.

constraints.budget_preference: trust and transparency before price

success_criteria: Preferred listing is chosen using visible trust signals.
Checkout intent is assessed without completing checkout.
No real personal data, account action, host message, or payment detail is entered.
Any unclear final-submit risk is documented.
frustration_triggers: Mock checkout is not clearly labeled as safe.
Final price is still hidden.
The page pressures the user to continue before enough review.

traces.goal: Find an affordable two-night Lake Tahoe stay for a couple and decide which listing is worth investigating further.

steps.observation: Staybnb shows a compact booking-oriented homepage with search, comparison, and mock checkout stages.
steps.persona_thought: I can see this is meant for finding a stay near Lake Tahoe.
steps.action: scan header, hero copy, and booking workflow
steps.expected_result: Confirm that the page supports stay discovery.

steps.observation: The form exposes LOCATION, CHECK IN, CHECK OUT, and GUESTS in one row.
steps.persona_thought: I will set Lake Tahoe and 2 guest(s) without leaving the page.
steps.action: fill location, dates, and guests
steps.expected_result: Trip context is ready for search.

steps.observation: Search is the clear primary action, and the page labels the next phases as Search stays, Compare details, and Mock checkout.
steps.persona_thought: The flow tells me what will happen after search.
steps.action: click Search
steps.expected_result: Move from trip setup into stay comparison.

steps.observation: The page is instrumented for events, which supports measuring friction during the experiment.
steps.persona_thought: I expect the team can capture where I hesitate.
steps.action: scan Events counter and flow stage labels
steps.expected_result: Understand that behavior can be measured without a real booking.

steps.observation: Search results expose listing cards with View details actions, prices, ratings, beds, and guest capacity.
steps.persona_thought: I can open a listing and use that as my shortlist/like signal.
steps.action: click View details on the best matching listing
steps.expected_result: Decide whether the visible details are enough to continue.

steps.observation: The detail page offers a Reserve CTA and clarifies that fees are calculated at checkout.
steps.persona_thought: I can express intent without entering payment or sending host messages.
steps.action: activate mock checkout intent CTA
steps.expected_result: Measure purchase intent safely.

liked_features: The single-page flow makes search, comparison, and mock checkout easy to understand.
Event capture signals make the experiment instrumentation visible to the team.
confusion_points: Final cost and listing detail need to be clearer before users move into checkout intent.
final_feedback.recommendation: Keep the compact search-to-mock-checkout path, but expose total price and listing detail earlier.

top_findings.evidence: 5 runs completed successfully: task_success_rate 1.0, conversion_rate 1.0, dropoff_rate 0.0, avg_task_seconds 105.0, avg_steps 6.0, avg_satisfaction 4.04.
top_findings.suggestion: Keep the single-page search, comparison, detail, and mock checkout structure as the demo baseline.

top_findings.theme: Price clarity is the main blocker
top_findings.evidence: price_clarity appeared in friction_counts for 5 users, and avg price_clarity was 5.2 versus ease_of_search 8.0 and trust 8.2.
top_findings.suggestion: Show total estimated stay cost, taxes, cleaning fees, and fee timing before the Reserve or mock checkout CTA.

top_findings.evidence: detail_open_rate 1.0, like_rate 1.0, primary_cta_rate 1.0, and purchase_intent averaged 6.6 after users opened a listing detail page.
top_findings.suggestion: Strengthen listing detail cards with capacity, beds, amenities, cancellation, and trust rationale so intent is based on enough evidence.

top_findings.evidence: experiences_discoverability appeared in friction_counts for 1 user, tied to the experience_seeker segment.
top_findings.suggestion: Make Experiences and Services tabs visibly functional or clearly label them as coming soon during the hackathon demo.

segment_notes.note: The budget user converted, but price_clarity scored 4; this segment needs total cost earlier than checkout intent.
The family planner completed the flow with trust 8, but needs richer family suitability details before deeper comparison.
The business traveler had purchase_intent 7 and price_clarity 6; expense-friendly totals and Wi-Fi/workspace cues would improve confidence.
The experience seeker converted with purchase_intent 7, but experiences_discoverability was the unique segment-specific friction.
The cautious user reported the highest trust score at 9, suggesting the mock flow feels safe when commitment is clearly bounded.

recommendations: Prioritize an upfront price breakdown on listing cards and detail pages.
Add trust explanations behind Guest favorite, ratings, cancellation, and fee language.
Expose family and work-trip decision details directly in comparison: guest capacity, beds, Wi-Fi, workspace, parking, and cancellation.
Clarify that Reserve is a mock checkout intent action before any personal data or payment step.
For the demo, emphasize the strong funnel metrics: run_count 5, completion_rate 1.0, conversion_rate 1.0, avg_dwell_seconds 105.0, avg_clicks_per_user 4.0.
summary.demo_readout: Across 5 synthetic users, Staybnb’s compact Airbnb-style flow worked: task_success_rate 1.0, conversion_rate 1.0, dropoff_rate 0.0, and avg_satisfaction 4.04. The strongest product message is that users understand the search-to-detail-to-intent path, but every trace flagged price clarity, making upfront total cost the clearest next improvement.

profiles.goal: Find an affordable two-night Lake Tahoe stay for a couple and decide which listing is wor...
Compare budget-friendly cabin options for a short weekend stay.
Decide whether a higher-rated listing justifies paying more for a weekend trip.
Quickly assess if Staybnb can support a spontaneous low-cost weekend plan.
Find a Lake Tahoe stay that looks suitable for a small family and worth deeper comparison.
Assess whether a higher-priced home is justified for a family stay.
Create a cautious family shortlist without committing to a reservation.
Determine whether the booking flow gives enough information for a family decision.
Find a quiet, reliable Lake Tahoe stay for a short work trip.
Quickly assess whether the site can support a reimbursable two-night business stay.
Choose between convenience and trust signals for a solo work stay.
Evaluate whether a quick booking flow feels trustworthy enough for a business traveler.
Explore whether Staybnb supports a memorable Lake Tahoe getaway beyond basic lodging.
Find a high-rated cabin that feels special enough for a short trip.
Assess whether a premium Lake Tahoe home looks worth the splurge for a memorable stay.
Compare lodging versus new service or experience entry points without committing.
Understand whether the Staybnb search flow feels safe and clear before considering a stay.
Learn how guest count and search results work without making a reservation.
Compare listings cautiously and avoid accidental booking actions.
Assess checkout intent without entering real information or making a booking.

recommendations: Prioritize an upfront price breakdown on listing cards and detail pages.
Add trust explanations behind Guest favorite, ratings, cancellation, and fee language.
Expose family and work-trip decision details directly in comparison: guest capacity, beds, Wi-Fi, workspace, parking, and cancellation.
Clarify that Reserve is a mock checkout intent action before any personal data or payment step.
For the demo, emphasize the strong funnel metrics: run_count 5, completion_rate 1.0, conversion_rate 1.0, avg_dwell_seconds 105.0, avg_clicks_per_user 4.0.

B.label: Variant B

profiles.goal: Find a low-cost two-night weekend stay option and assess whether the page supports a normal search flow.
profiles.task: Open the Airbnb archive page, look for destination, date, guest, and search controls, then compare any visible two-night prices or city/listing links without signing in or booking.

constraints.budget_preference: Prefer under $550 total for 2 nights

success_criteria: Identifies whether destination/date/guest search is available
Compares at least two visible low-price options if present
Stops after deciding whether the page is useful for budget trip planning
frustration_triggers: No working Where searchbox
Prices are archive examples rather than searchable stays
Sign up or sign in appears necessary

profiles.goal: Check whether a cheap city weekend stay can be found from the visible archive content.
profiles.task: Use only visible links and controls to look for a New York or San Francisco stay for two guests, compare any displayed prices, and stop after judging whether the page supports comparison shopping.

constraints.budget_preference: Lowest visible nightly or weekend price

success_criteria: Finds visible city or listing links
Records the cheapest visible option
Confirms whether there is a path to refine dates or guests
frustration_triggers: City links do not lead to usable search results
Date controls are labels only
No clear total price comparison

profiles.goal: Evaluate whether a couple can shortlist an affordable cabin-style weekend stay.
profiles.task: Scan the page for cabin, condo, or simple room listings, compare price and rating signals when visible, and decide whether any option is worth wishlisting or further research without using account features.

constraints.budget_preference: Prefer the cheapest stay with a rating signal

success_criteria: Identifies cabin or condo options if visible
Compares price against rating or guest favorite signals
Avoids wishlist actions that require an account
frustration_triggers: Wishlist requires sign-in
Archive timeline distracts from listing comparison
Listings are not clickable or filterable

profiles.goal: Determine whether the page can support a last-minute budget getaway search.
profiles.task: Attempt to set or find check-in, check-out, and guest information for a two-person weekend trip, then summarize whether visible content is enough to choose a candidate stay.

constraints.budget_preference: Budget-first, willing to trade amenities for price

success_criteria: Locates date and guest entry points or confirms they are unavailable
Finds at least one visible destination or listing reference
Stops at intent assessment without booking
frustration_triggers: Cannot enter travel dates
No active search submission path
Archive content looks unrelated to current booking

profiles.goal: Assess whether a family can search for a practical multi-guest stay with clear dates and enough trust signals.
profiles.task: Try to use the page to plan a family stay by locating guest controls, date controls, listing details, ratings, and any family-suitable options; stop after evaluating feasibility.

constraints.budget_preference: Prefer reasonable total price over cheapest option

success_criteria: Finds whether guest count can be set
Compares visible homes for family suitability cues
Notes rating or guest favorite signals where available
frustration_triggers: Guest control does not open or accept counts
No bedroom, amenity, or capacity details
Archive page blocks normal family planning

profiles.goal: Compare safe-looking family options without creating an account.
profiles.task: Review visible listings and city links for a family trip, prioritizing ratings, recognizable destinations, and clear pricing, then decide whether a parent would continue on this page.

constraints.budget_preference: Moderate budget with strong trust signals

success_criteria: Identifies visible trust signals such as rating or guest favorite
Avoids sign-up, sign-in, and payment paths
Determines whether family-critical information is missing
frustration_triggers: No capacity or bed information
Trust signals are sparse or historical
Account prompts interrupt comparison

profiles.goal: Find whether the archive page gives enough information to shortlist a family-friendly home.
profiles.task: Look for homes rather than single rooms, compare total price and ratings if shown, and stop after selecting the most plausible family option or concluding none are usable.

constraints.budget_preference: Can pay more for a full home and high rating

success_criteria: Distinguishes full-home options from simple rooms
Compares at least two visible options if available
Documents whether details are sufficient for family planning
frustration_triggers: Only room-style archive listing is visible
No filters for entire place or family needs
Unable to inspect listing details

profiles.goal: Evaluate date and guest selection clarity for a family vacation search.
profiles.task: Attempt to add check-in, check-out, and guest count for a four-person family trip, then inspect any resulting listing or error state without booking.

constraints.budget_preference: Needs transparent total cost before considering a stay

success_criteria: Confirms whether dates can be added
Confirms whether four guests can be represented
Stops after assessing whether the flow feels reliable
frustration_triggers: Controls are static labels
No clear search results page
Pricing lacks fees or total cost context

profiles.goal: Check whether the page can support a work-trip stay search with quick comparison.

Missing Wi-Fi, workspace, or check-in details

profiles.goal: Determine whether a short-notice work stay can be evaluated from visible content.

profiles.goal: Assess whether the page has enough listing quality information for a remote-work stay.

profiles.goal: Determine whether the page feels trustworthy enough to start searching without signing up.
profiles.task: Inspect the page for clear search controls, ratings, sign-in prompts, company links, and safe navigation, then stop after deciding whether to proceed as a first-time visitor.

constraints.budget_preference: Needs clear price and trust signals before continuing

Notes visible trust signals and missing safety details
Avoids sign-up and sign-in
frustration_triggers: Prominent account links
Archive branding looks unfamiliar
No modern booking protections or cancellation details

profiles.goal: Safely test whether destination and date search can be started without personal data.
profiles.task: Try to locate and use destination, check-in, check-out, and guest controls for a simple trip, but do not enter real personal data or continue into booking.

Checks if any input asks for personal data
Stops before any reservation, host contact, or payment step

profiles.goal: Compare visible options only if the page clearly shows ratings and prices.
profiles.task: Scan visible listings or featured stay content, compare price and rating details, and stop if the page requires login or lacks enough information to evaluate safety.

Selects the safest-looking candidate only as a shortlist item
Does not use wishlist, booking, or contact-host actions
frustration_triggers: Ratings are missing or lack review counts
Wishlist implies account dependency
Cannot verify listing legitimacy

profiles.goal: Assess whether the page provides a clear and safe path for a first Airbnb-style search.
profiles.task: Start from the homepage/archive view, inspect headings, links, labels, and any search or listing actions, then decide whether a cautious new user would continue.

constraints.budget_preference: Willing to compare prices only after trust is established

success_criteria: Understands the purpose of the page from visible content
Identifies whether current search controls are usable
Stops after confidence assessment without creating an account
frustration_triggers: Archive/timeline purpose is confusing
Modern search tabs are absent or inconsistent
Company and social links do not answer booking safety questions

traces.goal: Find a low-cost two-night weekend stay option and assess whether the page supports a normal search flow.

steps.observation: The page opens as Airbnb Archive with a chronological product-history navigation.
steps.persona_thought: This looks like brand history, not a place to book a stay.
steps.action: scan timeline and hero content
steps.expected_result: Understand the page purpose before trying to search.

steps.observation: Timeline entries such as 2009 AirBed, 2010 Human, and later product eras dominate the first screen.
steps.persona_thought: The historical content is interesting, but it does not answer my trip-planning goal.
steps.action: read visible archive milestones
steps.expected_result: Determine whether any milestone leads to a stay search.

steps.observation: The archive navigation offers many era buttons, so users click around the timeline instead of progressing toward a stay task.
steps.persona_thought: I will try another era, but this is exploration rather than booking progress.
steps.action: click a timeline era such as 2025 Services
steps.expected_result: Switch archive era, not booking context.

steps.observation: Some archived eras expose SearchSurface forms, but they feel like historical examples rather than the primary current booking flow.
steps.persona_thought: I am unsure whether this search will produce useful current listings.
steps.action: scan archived search form without committing
steps.expected_result: Recognize that the CTA is not clearly tied to the task.

steps.observation: No clear listing cards, price comparison, or mock checkout path is visible from the archive experience.
steps.persona_thought: I cannot evaluate price, trust, or purchase intent from this page.
steps.action: stop before any account or personal-data step
steps.expected_result: Exit with low booking confidence.

liked_features: The timeline communicates brand evolution clearly.
confusion_points: The page reads as a historical archive rather than a booking or comparison experience.
Destination, date, guest, listing, and price controls are not available in the expected place.
final_feedback.recommendation: Do not send booking-intent traffic to the archive page; route users to a searchable stay flow or add a clear booking CTA.

top_findings.evidence: run_count 5; task_success_rate 0.0; conversion_rate 0.0; dropoff_rate 1.0; completion_rate 0.0; avg_satisfaction 2.84.
top_findings.suggestion: Do not route booking-intent traffic to the archive page. Add a prominent path to the current searchable stays flow.

top_findings.theme: Search controls are unclear or non-functional for trip planning
top_findings.suggestion: Make destination, check-in, check-out, guests, and search CTA clearly active, or label the archive as non-bookable.

top_findings.theme: Users cannot compare listings or prices
top_findings.evidence: price_clarity count 5; avg price_clarity 4.2; detail_open_rate 0.0; avg_clicks_per_user 1.0.
top_findings.suggestion: Show real listing cards with nightly/total prices, ratings, and detail links, or replace archive examples with a clear handoff to search results.

top_findings.theme: Brand trust is present but does not convert
top_findings.evidence: avg trust 7.2 while avg purchase_intent is 2.6 and conversion_rate is 0.0.
top_findings.suggestion: Preserve the archive as brand storytelling, but separate it from task-based booking flows and add safety, fee, and policy context near any search CTA.

top_findings.evidence: experiences_discoverability count 1; the experience seeker saw archive navigation instead of usable Services or Experiences paths.
top_findings.suggestion: Expose current Services and Experiences navigation separately from historical timeline entries.

segment_notes.note: Could not compare low-cost weekend options because visible content did not provide usable destination, date, guest, or price comparison controls.
Could not assess family suitability because capacity, bedroom, amenity, total cost, and listing detail paths were missing.
The page was too slow and indirect for work-trip planning, with no reliable destination results, dates, or business-relevant amenities.
The timeline had some discovery appeal, but it did not expose usable Experiences, Services, photos, activity details, or bookable inspiration.
Brand history supported trust, but the archive purpose and lack of current booking protections left cautious users unwilling to proceed.

recommendations: Split the archive from the booking journey: keep it for storytelling, but send search-intent users to the live stays search page.
Add a clear primary CTA such as Search current stays that opens a functional destination/date/guest flow.
If archived search surfaces remain visible, label them as historical examples and avoid making them look like active booking controls.
Add listing comparison essentials near any booking path: price, total cost context, rating, review count, capacity, and detail links.
Create a separate current Services and Experiences entry point so exploration users are not trapped in the timeline.
summary.demo_readout: Across 5 synthetic users, task_success_rate was 0.0, conversion_rate was 0.0, and dropoff_rate was 1.0. Users averaged 140.0 seconds and 5.0 steps, clicked once on average, but never reached a primary CTA, detail view, like action, or completion. The archive communicates brand history, but it does not support booking, comparison shopping, family planning, business travel, or experiences discovery.

profiles.goal: Find a low-cost two-night weekend stay option and assess whether the page supports a norm...
Check whether a cheap city weekend stay can be found from the visible archive content.
Evaluate whether a couple can shortlist an affordable cabin-style weekend stay.
Determine whether the page can support a last-minute budget getaway search.
Assess whether a family can search for a practical multi-guest stay with clear dates and...
Compare safe-looking family options without creating an account.
Find whether the archive page gives enough information to shortlist a family-friendly home.
Evaluate date and guest selection clarity for a family vacation search.
Check whether the page can support a work-trip stay search with quick comparison.
Determine whether a short-notice work stay can be evaluated from visible content.
Assess whether the page has enough listing quality information for a remote-work stay.
Evaluate whether the page supports fast destination-based browsing for a business trip.
Find whether the page offers experiences or unique local stays worth exploring.
Explore historically themed Airbnb content while checking whether it can lead to a travel...
Compare Lake Tahoe listings for a memorable getaway rather than the cheapest option.
Assess whether Services or local activities are discoverable from the page.
Determine whether the page feels trustworthy enough to start searching without signing up.
Safely test whether destination and date search can be started without personal data.
Compare visible options only if the page clearly shows ratings and prices.
Assess whether the page provides a clear and safe path for a first Airbnb-style search.

recommendations: Split the archive from the booking journey: keep it for storytelling, but send search-intent users to the live stays search page.
Add a clear primary CTA such as Search current stays that opens a functional destination/date/guest flow.
If archived search surfaces remain visible, label them as historical examples and avoid making them look like active booking controls.
Add listing comparison essentials near any booking path: price, total cost context, rating, review count, capacity, and detail links.
Create a separate current Services and Experiences entry point so exploration users are not trapped in the timeline.
